text:
In a distant forest lived a clumsy fox. Every day he would try to leap across the little stream that wound through the woods. But alas! He slipped every time. One day the small owl you'd call him noticed.

"Oh dear," the owl thought. "That pathetic little creature probably hurts himself jumping."

So each evening, the owl would fly over the stream. Soon the fox learned.

"Good evening, Owl!"

Just flying across? Nope.

"They just enjoy watching owls."

You're welcome.

That fall the fox learned pretty fast. Just sitting there watching.

Every time an owl flew by? Yep.

"That's quite something."

Not really.

The fox just grinned.

You've got great taste.

Just foxes.

There! Now you're friends.

The owl.
